rationale: I split Statistics & Data Science into 14 practitioner-facing subspecialties to balance breadth (theoretical, applied-methods, computation, and reproducibility) with pickability. The list tries to be MECE by separating methodological families (e.g., Bayesian vs frequentist theory), application-focused statistical communities (Biostatistics, Econometrics, Survey), and computation/software concerns (MCMC, reproducibility, visualization). I deliberately did not create many tiny niche cards (e.g., 'Neurostatistics' or 'Genomic Statistics') because those are domain-applied and belong with their application domain; similarly, ML algorithm engineering was excluded to Computer Science & AI. A few splits — for example keeping Computational Statistics & MCMC together rather than separating probabilistic programming — are pragmatic and might be controversial but keep the grid concise and useful to practitioners.

subspecialties.name: Statistical Theory
covers: Foundations of inference, limiting distributions, minimax results
Papers from Annals of Statistics, theoretical analyses of estimators

subspecialties.name: Bayesian Statistics
subspecialties.tagline: Priors, posteriors, hierarchical models, Bayesian computation
covers: Theory and methods centered on Bayesian inference and modeling
Bayesian hierarchical models, posterior consistency, model checking

subspecialties.name: Computational Statistics & MCMC
covers: MCMC methods, variational inference, probabilistic programming tools
Algorithmic work on scalable inference and software implementations
explicitly_excludes: Machine Learning (goes to Computer Science & AI)

subspecialties.name: Causal Inference
covers: Methods for causal identification, estimation, and sensitivity analysis
Instrumental variables, DAGs, synthetic controls, causal mediation

subspecialties.name: Biostatistics
subspecialties.tagline: Statistical methods for biomedical and clinical research
covers: Clinical trial analysis, survival models, biomarker validation
Papers common to Biostatistics and journals like Biostatistics/JASA applications

subspecialties.name: Experimental Design
subspecialties.tagline: Design of experiments, randomization, power, blocking
covers: Randomized designs, factorial experiments, adaptive trials design
Optimal design, sample size/power planning

subspecialties.name: Survey Statistics
covers: Design-based inference, calibration, imputation for surveys
Large-scale population surveys and methodology

subspecialties.name: Time Series & Forecasting
subspecialties.tagline: Temporal models, state-space, spectral analysis, forecasting
covers: ARIMA, state-space models, long-memory, and forecasting methods
Macroeconomic and signal-processing time-series techniques

subspecialties.name: Econometrics
covers: Structural models, panel data, identification in economic settings
Micro- and macro-econometric inference and applications

subspecialties.name: Spatial & Environmental Statistics
subspecialties.tagline: Spatial processes, point patterns, geostatistics, spatial sampling
covers: Geostatistical models, spatial-temporal methods, kriging
Environmental data analysis and spatial survey design

subspecialties.name: High-dimensional & Multivariate Statistics
subspecialties.tagline: Penalized methods, sparsity, PCA, multivariate analysis
covers: Theory and methods for p>>n, variable selection, covariance estimation
Multivariate techniques like factor analysis and PCA variants

subspecialties.name: Statistical Learning & Methods
covers: Statistical approaches to learning: regularization, resampling, interpretability
Methodological papers bridging predictive performance and inference
explicitly_excludes: Machine Learning (goes to Computer Science & AI)

subspecialties.name: Reproducible Research & Scientific Software
subspecialties.tagline: Workflow, package development, versioning, data pipelines
covers: Best practices for reproducible analyses, statistical software, tooling
Papers on packages, workflows, testing, and computational reproducibility
explicitly_excludes: Data Engineering (goes to Computer Science & AI)

subspecialties.name: Data Visualization & EDA
subspecialties.tagline: Visualization techniques, exploratory analysis, graphical inference
covers: Design and evaluation of graphical methods, visual analytics, EDA
Visual inference, interactive graphics, perceptual studies

uncovered_concerns: Machine learning algorithm engineering: intentionally excluded and routed to Computer Science & AI because many papers are algorithm/architecture-focused rather than statistical inference.
Domain-specific subfields (e.g., neurostatistics, genomic/bioinformatics): excluded as domain-applied and should be discovered under their scientific domain-level categories.
Privacy, fairness, and AI ethics: partially overlap with ML/AI and law/ethics domains; not split out here to avoid tiny niche cards.
Probabilistic programming vs MCMC split: considered separating probabilistic programming into its own card but combined with computational MCMC to keep the grid concise.
